text: All right, good morning everyone. Am I audible? Yes, welcome. Good morning. So good morning to all our planning officers, representatives from various government agencies, colleagues from the ICD, and our partners in digital transformation. On behalf of the Government Digital Transformation Bureau, it is my pleasure to welcome you today's for today's orientation on the adoption of the new ISSP template. I'm Geraldine Bukog, but you can also call me G. I may be new to many of you as I took over the GDTB around three months ago. But even within that short time, one thing has become very clear to me: digital transformation cannot move forward without strong planning and committed planners behind it. As government continues to move toward a more digital, interconnected and citizen-centered future, the role of our planners becomes even more vital. More than preparing documents and compliance requirements, planners help shape our government agencies, innovate, adapt, and deliver better services to the Filipino people. The adoption of the new ISSP template is also aligned with the government's broader direction toward digital transformation and the implementation of the e-governance act. Through this initiative, we aim to strengthen alignment across government agencies, promote interoperability, improve resource planning, and ensure that digital investments lead to real and measurable public value. As planners, allow me to share a simple way of looking at ISSP not only as a process, but as a mindset that can guide us in our work. So, just like an ISSB, "I" is to imagine. Imagine the kind of government experience we want every Filipino to have. We want our future generation, our future children to have. To have an efficient, connected, responsive, and accessible government. S is to strategize. Strategize carefully by aligning technology, programs, and resources with your agency mandate, national directions, and the actual needs of our citizens. Second S is to simplify. Simplify systems and processes because digital transformation should make government services easier, faster, and more convenient for the public. And lastly, P is to perform with purpose and accountability, ensuring that every plan we create leads to meaningful outcomes and genuine impact. You, our planning officers, play a critical role in this transformation journey. Before systems are developed, before platforms are launched, and before services reach the public, it all begins with planning. It begins with you. Through your work, agencies are guided toward more strategic, sustainable, and impactful digital initiatives. More importantly, you help move government from fragmented and isolated efforts toward a more integrated, coordinated, and rule of government approach in delivering better and more responsive public services. So, we hope that today's orientation will provide clearer guidance on the new ISSP template and help equip everyone in crafting strategic and future ready digital transformation plans for your respective agencies. May we continue working together toward a smarter, more connected and digitally empowered Philippine government. Thank you very much and we wish everyone a productive and meaningful session.
